authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 436 - Disclosure Requirements and Prohibitions Concerning Franchising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the governing U.S. franchise disclosure rule. It defines the franchise record set and the federal baseline for supplier, territory, and disclosure obligations that constrain franchise-operations decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC's compliance guide is the official public explanation of franchise disclosure items, including Item 8 sourcing restrictions and Item 11 training, assistance, and required-system expectations that materially shape operations workflow boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supplier, training, and required-system prerequisite validation

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Fundamentals: Taking a deep dive into the Franchise Disclosure Document
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance translates FDD Items 8, 11, 12, and 20 into practical operating questions about required suppliers, advertising and training, territory restrictions, and current franchisee contacts that materially change franchise-operations handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Franchise artifact interpretation and terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Registration and Disclosure Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's guidelines are the recognized public workflow baseline for registration-state franchise disclosure and highlight where state filing, amendment, and relationship-law posture can materially change operations assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: Multi-Unit Commentary
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA's multi-unit commentary is the direct public workflow reference for area development, subfranchise, and multi-unit structures that alter unit counts, openings, supplier and fee disclosures, and ownership boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Policy Statement of the Federal Trade Commission on Franchisors' Use of Contract Provisions, Including Non-Disparagement, Goodwill, and Confidentiality Clauses
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC's July 12, 2024 policy statement is authoritative current guidance that franchise agreements and related workflows may not suppress regulator contact or hide unlawful fees, which materially affects fee and complaint escalation handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fee, complaint-rights, and retaliation-sensitive boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: A Guide to U.S. Retail Pricing Laws and Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST's Office of Weights and Measures publishes the authoritative public guide to state retail-pricing laws and regulations, which is directly relevant when franchise price books, shelf labels, promotions, or fee presentation create execution blockers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Price presentation and jurisdiction-sensitive execution review

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 1181 - Unit Pricing Guide - A Best Practice Approach to Unit Pricing (2025 Ed.)
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 1181 is the public control standard for clear, accurate, and usable unit pricing information across retail and e-commerce channels, making it relevant to franchise price-label, promo, and shelf-sign execution controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Pricing control and completion evidence sufficiency

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail product and location identifiers. The GS1 General Specifications are the core standard for how identification keys and barcodes should be used in retail and distribution workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Global Data Model
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GS1 Global Data Model is the recognized standard for the product attributes required to list, store, move, and sell products, which directly constrains franchise item-master, order, inventory, and product-content accuracy.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Product-data, order, and inventory evidence reconciliation

workflow_stages.name: Franchise context and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm brand, concept, jurisdiction, ownership model, unit scope, controlling FDD and agreement context, operations-manual revision, and authority map before accepting the work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supplier, product, and price-book prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify approved supplier status, item or menu-code mappings, price-book version, fee presentation, promotion schedule, and unit applicability before execution progresses.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Order, receiving, and inventory reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Review POS, OMS, ERP, receiving, return, and inventory records to determine whether order and stock status can safely advance or require exception handling.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Pricing, fee, and promotion boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve price presentation, unit-pricing, fee disclosure, and promotion controls and stop when the request would require legal or pricing-approval authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and franchise-specific rework progression
workflow_stages.description: Move supplier, inventory, pricing, return, or territory-sensitive issues into blocked or rework states with explicit evidence and next-owner routing rather than silent closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and multi-unit closure control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when unit-level execution evidence, system-of-record status, and any stricter tenant controls align across the affected franchise scope.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: current Franchise Disclosure Document, franchise agreement, state addenda, and operations-manual revision
approved supplier lists, required-product standards, and territory or protected-market records
item master, SKU, PLU, GTIN, barcode, and menu-code mappings
price books, mandatory fee configuration, promotion calendars, coupon rules, and shelf or signage references
purchase orders, transfer orders, invoices, receiving logs, return authorizations, and vendor case notes
POS transactions, OMS events, ERP or back-office records, inventory counts, and stock-adjustment history
field-visit notes, brand-standard findings, exception approvals, service tickets, and completion confirmations
systems_of_record: document repository for FDD, agreements, addenda, and operations manuals

allowed: Classify franchise execution requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare execution updates, completion records, workflow summaries, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required franchise, unit, supplier, pricing, order, inventory, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires legal interpretation, supplier approval, pricing approval, fee approval, finance or royalty approval, or regulated-product authority.
FTC, state, standards, or tenant policy sources materially conflict on price presentation, fee handling, completion criteria, or ownership.
A promotion, mandatory fee, supplier restriction, or territory issue changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without unit-level or system-level completion evidence.
Approve new suppliers, unsupported substitutions, or permanent pricing or fee changes without documented authority.
Treat the franchise industry label as cosmetic and answer with generic retail operations language that ignores FDD, operating-manual, approved-supplier, or multi-unit constraints.

domain_failure_modes: the lane closes a pricing, order, or promotion case even though unit-level completion evidence is missing
a non-approved supplier or product substitution is treated as an execution convenience instead of an approval-bound decision
price-book, fee, or promo mismatches are resolved ad hoc without checking franchise disclosures, operating-manual constraints, or state pricing rules
POS, OMS, ERP, receiving, and inventory records conflict and the lane still claims completion
the lane drifts into legal interpretation of agreements, territory rights, or state addenda
multi-unit or protected-territory context is ignored and a generic chain-retail answer is given
tenant-specific stricter controls are omitted even though they materially change the workflow

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State franchise relationship laws, state pricing laws, and category-specific regulated-product rules can materially narrow the public baseline for a particular brand or unit.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, NASAA, NIST, and GS1 sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, product category, and tenant operating policy before applying a state-specific or category-specific conclusion.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant operating manuals, approved-supplier rules, promo-approval chains, and company-owned versus franchised unit treatment can materially change who owns an exception.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline defines the boundary posture, but runtime must retrieve tenant authority matrices and unit-ownership rules before resolving ambiguous execution ownership.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling instead of treating franchise operations as generic store support.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdiction and tenant-specific variation are explicitly handled through retrieval and escalation rules.
The lane stays inside execution-state handling for product, pricing, promotion, order, return, and inventory workflows and refuses legal, pricing-approval, supplier-approval, and finance authority.
